title: Siddha Bhairavi
tagline: Siddha Bhairavi — spiritual town of Odisha

overview.short: Siddha Bhairavi temple is a shrine situated at Mantridi in Ganjam district of Odisha India.
overview.description: Siddha Bhairavi temple is a shrine situated at Mantridi in Ganjam district of Odisha India. The presiding deity is the goddess Bhairavi. Carved in crude fashion, the idol features one leg and four hands. It is said that this idol was excavated from a ploughed field.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Bahadur Pentho
topPlaces.description: Bahadur Pentho is a village in Ganjam district in the south of Odisha, India, bordering Andhra Pradesh. It is located southeast of Berhampur and northeast of Ichchapuram.

topPlaces.name: Govinda puram
topPlaces.description: Govinda puram is a village in Ganjam district in the south of Odisha, India, bordering Andhra Pradesh. It is located southeast of Berhampur and northeast of Ichchapuram.

topPlaces.name: Biswanathpur
topPlaces.description: Biswanathpur is a village in Ganjam district in the south of Odisha, India, bordering Andhra Pradesh. This village owes its name to Vishwanath Dev Gajapati, the Suryavanshi king of Kalinga who founded this place after conducting a royal ancient ritual known as 'Vajapeya Yagna'.

topPlaces.name: Golanthara
topPlaces.description: Golanthara is a village located in the southern part of Ganjam District of Odisha, India.

topPlaces.name: Siddha Vinayaka
topPlaces.description: Panchama is a small village famous for the temple of Panchama Ganesh, or Siddha Vinayaka as it is locally known. Legend says that the Gajapati King Purushottama deva (15th century) of Puri on the way to his conquest of the Kanchi Kingdom recovered the image in the hollow of two trees of Bara and Ashwattha and paid his homage to the image after its consecration.

topPlaces.name: Kanisi, Berhampur
topPlaces.description: Kanisi is one of the 23 Tahasils in Ganjam district in the Indian state of Odisha.

topPlaces.name: Surla
topPlaces.description: Surla is a village in Chikti Block of Ganjam district, Odisha, India at the Andhra Pradesh-Odisha border on National Highway 5. It sits across from Ichchapuram, in Andhra Pradesh.

items.note: Bahadur Pentho is a village in Ganjam district in the south of Odisha, India, bordering Andhra Pradesh. It is located southeast of Berhampur and northeast of Ichchapuram.
Govinda puram is a village in Ganjam district in the south of Odisha, India, bordering Andhra Pradesh. It is located southeast of Berhampur and northeast of Ichchapuram.

Biswanathpur is a village in Ganjam district in the south of Odisha, India, bordering Andhra Pradesh. This village owes its name to Vishwanath Dev Gajapati, the Suryavanshi king of Kalinga who founded this place after conducting a royal ancient ritual known as 'Vajapeya Yagna'.
Golanthara is a village located in the southern part of Ganjam District of Odisha, India.

Panchama is a small village famous for the temple of Panchama Ganesh, or Siddha Vinayaka as it is locally known. Legend says that the Gajapati King Purushottama deva (15th century) of Puri on the way to his conquest of the Kanchi Kingdom recovered the image in the hollow of two trees of Bara and Ashwattha and paid his homage to the image after its consecration.
Kanisi is one of the 23 Tahasils in Ganjam district in the Indian state of Odisha.
Surla is a village in Chikti Block of Ganjam district, Odisha, India at the Andhra Pradesh-Odisha border on National Highway 5. It sits across from Ichchapuram, in Andhra Pradesh.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Siddha Bhairavi?
faq.a: The best time to visit Siddha Bhairavi is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Siddha Bhairavi?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Biju Patnaik International Airport, Bhubaneswar (161 km) and the nearest railway station is Puri Railway Station (131 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Siddha Bhairavi?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Siddha Bhairavi at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Siddha Bhairavi?
faq.a: Stays in Siddha Bhairavi start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Siddha Bhairavi?
faq.a: The top attractions are Bahadur Pentho, Govinda puram, Biswanathpur, Golanthara, Siddha Vinayaka.
